name: Archaeological site La Cava – circular via Garcinarro and the La Vega river
name_original: Yacimiento arqueológico La Cava – circular por Garcinarro y el río de La Vega
region_intro: Hiking around El Valle de Altomira offers exploration of a tranquil region characterized by diverse landscapes between Guadalajara and Cuenca. The terrain features mountain slopes, well-preserved Mediterranean forests, and notable limestone formations such as torcas. The Sierra de Altomira provides expansive views of the Alcarria region, with elevations suitable for varied hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around El Valle de Altomira The most popular hiking route is Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46), a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route features unique sculptures carved into sandstone rocks and offers views of the Buendía reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino de Zorita and Recópolis Loop, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.6 km) path. This trail explores historical areas and varied natural scenery, typically completed in 3 hours 57 minutes. Local hikers also love the Altomira Trail, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Altomira, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around El Valle de Altomira is defined by Mediterranean forests, limestone formations, and panoramic views from the Sierra de Altomira. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in El Valle de Altomira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore El Valle de Altomira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Valle de Altomira?
A: El Valle de Altomira offers a selection of 14 hiking routes. These trails cater to various preferences, with 2 easy routes and 12 moderate options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in El Valle de Altomira?
A: The terrain in El Valle de Altomira is diverse, featuring well-preserved Mediterranean forests, distinctive limestone formations like torcas, and mountain slopes. You'll find a mix of landscapes, from serene woodlands to expansive vistas from the Sierra de Altomira.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in El Valle de Altomira?
A: Yes, the region offers family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012671860/route-of-the-faces-circular-from-buendia-pr-cu-46" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46)&lt;/a&gt; is a short and easy route, perfect for families. It features unique sculptures carved into sandstone rocks within a pine forest, making it an engaging experience for all ages.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in El Valle de Altomira?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting landmarks. The unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; near Buendía features allegorical sculptures carved into sandstone. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3235056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken&lt;/a&gt;. For history enthusiasts, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3527077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recópolis Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3527044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zorita de los Canes Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Valle de Altomira?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012671860/route-of-the-faces-circular-from-buendia-pr-cu-46" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242576983/camino-de-altomira-pr-cu-40-circular-desde-saceda-trasierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Altomira (PR-CU 40)&lt;/a&gt;, which starts from Saceda-Trasierra and leads to the summit of the Sierra de Altomira.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Valle de Altomira?
A: El Valle de Altomira offers beautiful hiking experiences throughout the year. In summer, the mountain slopes are vibrant with yellow sunflower fields, creating a stunning visual. The well-preserved Mediterranean forest makes spring and autumn particularly pleasant for hiking, with mild temperatures and rich natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in El Valle de Altomira?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in El Valle de Altomira, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, including Mediterranean forests and limestone formations, as well as the panoramic views from the Sierra de Altomira.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering scenic panoramas?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Altomira provides breathtaking 360-degree views of the entire Alcarria region. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another spot where you can enjoy expansive vistas of the surrounding natural beauty.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in El Valle de Altomira?
A: Hiking durations vary depending on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012671860/route-of-the-faces-circular-from-buendia-pr-cu-46" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46)&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242639649/altomira-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altomira Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 3 hours.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in El Valle de Altomira?
A: While specific parking areas for every trailhead are not detailed, the Buendía Dam area, which provides access to routes like the Hermitage of the Virgen de los Desamparados, features well-equipped picnic areas and facilities, suggesting parking availability. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7012012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Motorhome Park&lt;/a&gt; also indicates facilities for visitors in the vicinity.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the hiking routes in El Valle de Altomira?
A: The hiking routes in El Valle de Altomira are primarily categorized as easy and moderate. Out of the 14 available trails, 2 are considered easy, and 12 are moderate, making the region accessible for a wide range of hiking abilities.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can explore historical areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025712993/camino-de-zorita-and-recopolis-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Zorita and Recópolis Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that takes you through historical areas, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3527077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recópolis Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of nature and history.
tips: La Cava is a 12-hectare multiphase archaeological site that was first occupied 4,000 years ago, during the Bronze Age (between 2,500 and 1,000 BC). It is located on top of a hill whose western front is almost inaccessible, with the Sierra de Altomira in the background.

Small and quiet town in Cuenca that is usually taken as a starting point to visit the archaeological site of La Cava a few kilometers from here. It is also interesting to visit the Church of Nuestra Señora del Sagrario, which is located in the center of town.
